About: It is about the concept of pornocracy.
It is about the woman of his time: a femme fatale who was increasingly assertive, ruthless and seductive.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Decadent movement.
It is associated with the concept of pornocracy.
It is associated with the Theophylacti family.
ConsistsOf: It consists of gouache and watercolor paint heightened with pastel on paper.
CreatedBy: It was created by the Belgian artist Félicien Rops.
HasA: It has black shoes and stockings.
It has a band of gold and blue silk.
It has three winged putti flying away.
It has a frieze depicting four allegories of the arts.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a woman holding a swine on a leash.
It is characterized by a woman who is almost naked.
It is characterized by three putti flying away in shock or horror.
It is characterized by a frieze depicting the allegories of the arts.
It is characterized by the victory of sensuousness and eroticism.
It is characterized by the boredom of the academic art of that era.
HeldIn: It was held in other museum collections.
It was held in the Musée provincial Félicien Rops in Namur, Belgium.
It was held in the LACMA.
It was held in the National Museum of Western Art.
It was held in the Rijksmuseum Amsterdam.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Pierre-Joseph Proudhon's anti-feminist tract La Pornocratie ou les femmes dans les temps modernes.
IsA: It is a work executed in gouache and watercolor paint heightened with pastel on paper.
It is considered the best-known work by Rops.
KnownAs: It is known as La dame au cochon.
It is known as The Lady with the Pig.
KnownFor: It is known for being the best-known work by Rops.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Cercle des XX.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the 1886 exhibition of the Cercle des XX.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the first half of the 10th century.
PartOf: It is part of the collection of the Musée provincial Félicien Rops in Namur, Belgium.
PublishedAs: It was published as soft-ground etchings.
